title: The Broken Sundial
subtitle: Chapter 02 · The Wider Fractures
introduction: A quiet, overgrown monastery courtyard has a grand stone sundial at its center. Focus on the shadow.
description: The shadow cast by the sundial's copper gnomon slides across the stone markers, indicating exactly 12:00 PM (Noon) three seconds before the sun reaches its zenith.
observation: The shadow slides forward, counting the noon hour early while the sun is still suspended in the eastern sky.
reconstruction: Carry the copper gnomon, the stone courtyard, and the carved noon marker.
attunements: The metal gnomon is hot, carrying high solar radiation despite the shadow offset.
The stone courtyard is cool, reflecting an early morning temperature.
The noon marker shows a deep physical indentation from years of shadow casting.
revelation:
What was wrong: the shadow marked the noon hour while the sun remained suspended in the eastern sky.

Why it mattered: the monastery's daily prayers had kept the sequence of hours locked, pulling the shadow forward ahead of the sky's actual motion.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not there. Focus on the tip of the shadow on the stone dial.
anomaly_definition.success_text: Solar desynchronization detected. The shadow moves independently of the sun's position.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE SHADOW CROSSES THE NOON MARKER.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE COPPER GNOMON IS HOT. The metal contains high thermal energy, despite the early shadow alignment.
THE COURTYARD STONES ARE COOL. The ground retains the cool temperature of the early morning hours.
THE NOON MARKER CARRIES PHYSICAL WEAR. A deep groove is worn into the stone from decades of shadow contact.
